name: Safeport Law
description_short: Attorney-backed credit repair service offering bureau disputes, score tracking, and a 90-day money-back guarantee through a network of licensed attorneys.
description_long:
Safeport Law is a credit repair law firm operating as Coleman Legal, LLC, headquartered in LaGrange, Georgia. Founded in 2022 and BBB accredited since March 2023, the company markets attorney-backed credit dispute services to consumers across all 50 states, DC, Puerto Rico, the US Virgin Islands, and Armed Forces addresses. Despite marketing language referencing "over 15 years of experience," this appears to reflect the combined professional backgrounds of affiliated attorneys rather than the company's own operating history. Safeport Law also offers debt relief services alongside credit repair, positioning itself as a one-stop shop for consumers dealing with both damaged credit and unresolved debt obligations.

Safeport Law operates a straightforward three-step intake process: clients provide their information and complete a free credit pull, the team identifies damaging items across all three bureaus, and the attorneys begin disputing and challenging the credit profile. The company offers two service tiers — Credit Cleanse and Credit Cleanse+. The base Credit Cleanse plan covers bureau disputes, credit score analysis, and a score tracker for $89.99 per month with an $89 initial work fee. Credit Cleanse+ adds creditor interventions and inquiry targeting for $129.99 per month with a $129 initial work fee. The initial work fee is charged six days after signup, and monthly billing begins one month after registration. Both tiers include access to the client portal at portal.safeportlaw.com, which provides 24/7 progress tracking.

The primary differentiator for Safeport Law is that disputes are filed by a network of licensed attorneys rather than paralegals or non-attorney staff. This gives their correspondence additional legal weight and signals a higher level of accountability compared to non-law-firm credit repair companies. The company's 90-day money-back guarantee provides meaningful consumer protection: if no negative items are removed from your credit report after 90 days of active, paying participation, a full refund can be requested. Both plans also include a free initial consultation with a credit pull, so consumers can review their situation before committing to a monthly plan.

Despite these strengths, Safeport Law carries notable risks that prospective clients should weigh carefully. The company is only about three years old, which limits the verifiable track record available to evaluate long-term effectiveness. Consumer reviews report difficulty canceling the service, with some users describing hold times exceeding an hour when attempting to cancel by phone. Several customers have reported disputes over the 90-day money-back guarantee, claiming it was not honored despite no results — the fine print requires uninterrupted, active participation for the full 90 days, and any lapse voids eligibility. Pricing is not transparently listed on the website and loads dynamically, a practice flagged as a red flag by some reviewers. Credential transparency is also weak — no specific attorney names or bar numbers are publicly disclosed on the site, which makes it difficult for consumers to independently verify the legal team behind the service.

pricing.guarantee_details: 90-day money-back guarantee: if no negative items are removed from your credit report after 90 days of active participation, you may apply for a full refund. You must remain an active, paying participant for the entire 90-day period — canceling or missing payments during this window voids the guarantee. Refund eligibility begins at day 90, not before.

90-day money-back guarantee provides a risk-reduction safety net for skeptical consumers
Free initial consultation with a credit pull included at no cost
Serves all 50 states plus DC, Puerto Rico, US Virgin Islands, and Armed Forces addresses
BBB accredited with an A rating and no CFPB complaints on file
Both credit repair and debt relief services available under one roof
24/7 progress tracking via a dedicated online client portal

Cancellation reported as difficult, with hold times exceeding one hour by phone
Some users report the 90-day money-back guarantee was not honored despite no results
Pricing not transparently displayed on website — loads dynamically, which some consumers flag as a red flag
No named attorneys or bar numbers publicly disclosed — weak credential transparency
best_for: Consumers who want attorney-backed disputes for added legal weight vs. non-law-firm alternatives
People dealing with both damaged credit and outstanding debt who want a single provider
Those who have had poor results with non-law-firm credit repair companies
Consumers who want inquiry-targeted disputes in addition to standard bureau challenges
diagnosis: Best suited for consumers who want attorney-backed credit repair and can afford $90–$130 per month. The 90-day money-back guarantee offers some protection, but read the fine print carefully — active, uninterrupted enrollment for the full 90 days is required to qualify.
services: Free credit pull and initial consultation
Credit score analysis and identification of damaging items
Attorney-filed dispute letters to all three credit bureaus (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion)
Credit score tracking and ongoing progress monitoring
Inquiry targeting and removal (Credit Cleanse+ plan only)
Debt relief services alongside credit repair
24/7 online client portal access at portal.safeportlaw.com
90-day money-back guarantee administration and refund processing
typical_results_timeline: Initial dispute responses and first removals may appear within 45–90 days. Meaningful credit score improvement typically requires 3–6 months of active enrollment, depending on the number, age, and severity of negative items on the credit report.
